query: CRT Mexico boletín mayo junio 2026 líneas registradas CURP cifras actualizadas padrón telefonía
answer:
## Research Report: CRT Mexico — Padrón de Telefonía Móvil / CURP Registration (Mayo–Junio 2026)

---

### 1. Background and Legal Framework

Mexico's **Comisión Reguladora de Telecomunicaciones (CRT)** — which replaced the former Instituto Federal de Telecomunicaciones (IFT) — is administering a mandatory registration of all mobile phone lines to the holder's **CURP** (Clave Única de Registro de Población) for individuals, or **RFC** for companies. The legal basis is Articles 103 and 164 of the *Ley en Materia de Telecomunicaciones y Radiodifusión* (LMTR), published in the DOF on July 16, 2025, with implementing guidelines (*Lineamientos para la Identificación de Líneas Telefónicas Móviles*) published December 9, 2025.

- **Registration period**: January 9, 2026 – June 30, 2026
- **Firm deadline**: June 30, 2026 — CRT has publicly stated **"no habrá prórrogas"** (no extensions)
- **Consequence of non-compliance**: Starting **July 1, 2026**, unregistered lines will be **temporarily suspended** (only emergency calls or regularization calls allowed), with potential permanent deactivation thereafter
- **Per-person limit**: Each CURP can register up to **10 lines**
- **Scope**: All active mobile lines — prepaid, postpaid, physical SIM, and eSIM. Postpaid/contract lines are being registered automatically by operators; the manual obligation falls primarily on **prepaid users**

---

### 2. Total Mobile Lines in Mexico (Baseline Discrepancies)

There is a significant discrepancy in the total line count depending on the source:

| Figure | Source | Date |
|---|---|---|
| **144,585,131** | CRT official sector data (Q3 2025) | Published April 23, 2026 |
| **152.5 million** | CRT / former IFT last report | Cited ~2026 |
| **158.9 million** | The CIU (Competitive Intelligence Unit), Q1 2026 | May 2026 |
| **161 million** | Expansión, citing CRT/market data | April 2026 |
| **162.2 million** | The CIU, Q4 2025 (pre-registration baseline) | Late 2025 |

The CRT's official 144.5M figure counts only lines with voice traffic during the measured quarter, while The CIU's higher figures include all active SIMs. This materially affects compliance percentages.

**Market share** (CRT Q3 2025):
- América Móvil (Telcel): **57.90%**
- AT&T: **16.42%**
- Telefónica (Movistar): **14.05%**
- Grupo Walmart (Bait): **7.04%**

---

### 3. Registration Progress — Chronological Data

| Date | Lines Registered | % Compliance | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| March 8, 2026 | 20.4 million | ~12.6% | Expansión |
| March 25, 2026 | 22 million | ~14% | El Universal |
| April 19, 2026 | 30.2 million | ~19.8–20.3% | CRT via El País, El Imparcial |
| May 13, 2026 | 48 million | ~33% | CRT via El Financiero |
| May 19, 2026 | **49,541,262** | ~34% | La Jornada (precise CRT figure) |
| May 31, 2026 | 52.3 million | ~36.2% | Periódico Correo |
| **June 1, 2026** | **52,376,625** | **~36.2%** (of 144.5M) | WRadio, El CEO |

**Key acceleration**: Between April 19 and May 13 (24 days), 18 million new lines were added (~750,000/day), largely driven by operators auto-registering postpaid lines.

**Current daily rate** (late May): ~250,000 lines/day.

**Required rate to hit 100% by June 30**: The CIU calculates approximately **3 million lines/day** — a figure they consider **unrealistic**.

---

### 4. Latest Snapshot (as of June 1–2, 2026)

- **52,376,625 lines registered** out of ~144.5 million = **~36.2% compliance**
- **~92–96 million lines remain unregistered**
- **29 days remaining** until the June 30 deadline
- CRT's official Twitter (@CRTGobMX) posted June 1: *"Estamos a 29 días de que finalice el registro de líneas celulares"*

---

### 5. Extension / Prórroga — Conflicting Signals

- **Official CRT position (as of May 28–30)**: Ricardo Castañeda, Director General de Política Regulatoria: *"No tenemos sobre la mesa llevar a cabo alguna prórroga porque es un plazo establecido en la Ley."*
- **Contrary report (June 2, 2026)**: Periódico Correo reported that *"fuentes cercanas a la CRT"* indicate the government is **analyzing a possible extension** to avoid mass suspension of millions of lines — contradicting the public stance.
- **Industry pressure**: Carlos Slim has questioned the mandatory registration and called for it to be *"re-studied"*.

---

### 6. Market Impact

- **3.26 million users disconnected** in Q1 2026 (The CIU) — the market dropped from 162.2M lines (Q4 2025) to 158.9M (Q1 2026). Attributed to user fear of the registration mandate.
- **Mobile telecom revenues Q1 2026**: 95,528 million pesos, up **6.8% YoY** despite line losses.
- A **black market for pre-registered SIM cards** has emerged, undermining the registry's stated security objectives (Magnanet, May 2026).
- Operators (Telcel, AT&T, Movistar, OMVs) launched a **joint campaign** in April 2026 to push registrations, criticizing CRT's lack of official communication strategy.

---

### 7. Key CRT Officials

- **Norma Solano Rodríguez** — Comisionada Presidenta de la CRT
- **Ricardo Castañeda** — Director General de Política Regulatoria de la CRT

---

key_facts: Mexico's CRT mandatory mobile line registration (CURP linkage) runs January 9 – June 30, 2026, with no official extension announced as of early June 2026.
As of June 1, 2026, 52,376,625 lines have been registered — approximately 36.2% of the CRT's official baseline of 144.5 million active lines.
An estimated 92–96 million lines remain unregistered with only 29 days until the June 30 deadline.
The CIU calculates that ~3 million registrations per day would be needed to reach full compliance by June 30 — deemed unrealistic given the current rate of ~250,000/day.
3.26 million mobile users disconnected in Q1 2026 (market dropped from 162.2M to 158.9M lines), attributed to fear of the registration mandate.
CRT officially states 'no habrá prórrogas' (no extensions), but Periódico Correo reported on June 2 that sources close to CRT say the government is analyzing a possible extension.
Starting July 1, 2026, unregistered lines face temporary suspension — limited to emergency and regularization calls only.
Each CURP can register up to 10 mobile lines; postpaid lines are being auto-registered by operators.
Telcel holds 57.9% market share, followed by AT&T (16.4%), Movistar (14.1%), and Bait (7.0%).
Mobile telecom revenues in Q1 2026 were 95,528 million pesos, up 6.8% YoY despite the line losses.
A black market for pre-registered SIM cards has emerged, undermining the registry's security objectives.
